residents_comments: Resident visually noted as having bugs on them, getting the resident help through the senior program, residents unit treated for bed bugs and scheduled for a second treatment, and resident received a violation letter for the condition of their unit.
Replacement of the American flag
No additional resident comments under Section XI (None)
executive_directors_report: MDE Waste-Water Sampling Program: The Board discussed the Covid study on water sampling at Attick Towers. The program started on 3/29/21. There have been seven samplings. Covid has been detected at every sampling. The HACCP receives reports every Friday. Currently there is not enough data, and sampling is scheduled indefinitely. It has been recommended that a letter could be sent to residents informing them of the results and recommendations.

Waiting List: There were 27 individuals on the waiting list. Everyone was contacted, and there are only 4 interested/qualified participants. It was recommended that instead of opening the waiting list, the Board could solicit participants from referrals through the P.G County Dept. of Social Services/Homeless Division. There are an abundance of senior/disabled individuals in the shelter that could benefit from receiving residency at Attick Towers. The Board discussed the amendments to the ACOP and scheduled public hearings to accomplish this task.

Vending Machines: The residents responded through the survey; they approve of the installation of a vending machine. The Board discussed the various options the vending machine would be purchased, as well as the proceeds distributed.
Comcast: Concerning the follow up of WIFI internet services. Comcast, as well as Verizon only had options where residents will have to pay individual costs for internet in Attick Towers.
Training: The Board discussed leadership training for all commissioners. HUD is offering free training to Housing Authority commissioners. Commissioners will also receive job descriptions.
ACOP changes submission date extended until June. Changes will be discussed. The Board suggested they could meet at the office, in the conference room.

New security service company started on Tuesday, 4/20/21. Officer Wyatt was assigned to Attick Towers. Introduction letters have been sent to all residents. It was noted things have gotten better since the day/live-in officers were hired.
ROSS Grant: Selected to receive funding. The funding amount was $249,000 over a period of time. The funding would be utilized for hiring a coordinator for elderly/disabled services.
decisions: Motion to approve the Agenda as submitted was unanimously approved by a vote of 5-0 (moved by Ms. Lynn, seconded by Mr. McFadden).
Motion to approve changes to the Agenda was approved (moved by Ms. Lynn, seconded by Mr. Catlin).
Mayor Wojahn swore in Ms. Elizabeth Lynn as the official Resident Commissioner of Attick Towers.
Motion to approve the financial reports as submitted was unanimously approved by a vote of 5-0 (moved by Ms. Lynn, seconded by Ms. Keeler).
Motion to approve the regular monthly meeting minutes from March 25, 2021 was unanimously approved by a vote of 5-0 (moved by Mr. Catlin, seconded by Ms. Lynn).
Board reviewed and discussed invoices over $2,000: TK Elevator invoice for $2,419.26 for elevator contract; TK Elevator invoice for $3,080.95 for elevator maintenance contract.
Mr. McFadden will draft a thank-you letter to delegates for the $2 million grant.
Delete 52723 Board Resolution from the agenda.
Board to solicit waiting list participants from referrals through P.G. County Dept. of Social Services/Homeless Division.
ACOP changes submission date extended until June with public hearings scheduled.
Next Meeting scheduled for May 27, 2021.

executive_directors_report: Funding: The eLOCCS (Line of Credit Control System) payment system, capital operation, working on bank account at SunTrust to switch over, paperwork, Mr. Simpson information, forms, and the pull down of $40K.

Focusing on roach infestations of code enforcement units. One unit with bedbugs treated successfully.

Vendors: Received a bid for three units with extensive plumbing issues. There will be an RFP sent out. No active residents living in the three units, and HUD is being requested to approve taking the units off line until completely repaired.
Putting a HUB in Attick Towers: A police HUB is being considered to be installed at Attick Towers.

ABC Company: The program established by ABC has enrolled 18 people and 2 more residents are needed to comply. Residents need to be enrolled in the Medicaid program. Another enrollment period planned to get 20 Medicaid compliant residents.

Motion to adjourn the regular monthly meeting and go into executive session - approved unanimously 4-0 by Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez. Meeting adjourned at 12:15 p.m.
Board reviewed and discussed Housing Authority financial statements.
Board reviewed and approved minutes from the regular monthly meeting held on January 23 - approved unanimously 4-0.

residents_comments: None
executive_directors_report: Courtesy Officers: Two courtesy officers have been chosen to work at Attick Towers. One officer will receive $25 per hour wage working between 10am-6pm. The second officer would be a live-in officer working between 6pm-2am. Both officers will come in on Wednesday for background check and application. Officers would apply for special police certification after being hired by the HACCP, taking approximately 90 days after application submission.
Misconduct and behavioral issues with various residents. Disturbances have been documented and residents are receiving notices.
People coming into the building washing clothes. One specific incident in which a trespasser had to be removed by police officers. Intruders may be acquaintances of residents.

Budget items: Cameras will be installed (in front of building, laundry room, and blind spots), physical needs assessment projects, accountant putting together salaries, additional positions, repairs of first floor, repairs denied by insurance company because of delayed submission of claims, and Board suggestions of further budget items.

Covid-19 testing: Twenty-six residents at Attick Towers participated in Covid testing given in the building. Residents had to utilize their medical insurance to cover the costs. Tests were only approved for residents.

Parking: The Board reviewed a spreadsheet showing costs incurred by HACCP from 2018-2019. Total cost incurred was $11,793.00 for engineering services, County permits and inspections. A construction permit due for renewal at a cost of $303.00 has been expired since June 2019. The Board has 60 days to renew the permit. The permit is filed under the name McEntire Property. Ms. Johnson and Ms. Perez will investigate further.
The Board discussed that all Commissioners should be on the same page concerning parking plans and expansion of a second building at Attick Towers.

Free meals to residents: The Senior Service Nutrition Program of P.G. County will be supplying residents at Attick Towers with frozen meals twice a month until December. Angie is arranging transportation for residents to get food from food pantries.
RFP Legal Services: RFPs are being solicited to be obtained by the HACCP. All legal services will only be utilized by the company contracted with.

T-Mobile: The contract with T-Mobile is put on hold until further notice. T-Mobile and Sprint are merging and will be determining which sites they would like to move forward with for tower installation.
Retreat: The Board discussed the importance of having a retreat, either in person or remotely through Zoom. The retreat is an open meeting and must follow open meeting guidelines.

Lease changes and updates were discussed. Ms. Johnson noted she needs the Board to submit any changes or revisions including increase in rent, negligence in utility usage by residents, and three-year leases by the beginning of the fiscal year.
decisions: Motion made by Ms. Perez and seconded by Mr. McFadden to approve the financial report as submitted. Unanimously approved 4-0 by Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez.

Motion made by Ms. Lynn and seconded by Mr. McFadden to approve the regular monthly meeting minutes from June 25, 2020 as amended. Unanimously approved 4-0 by Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez.

Motion made by Ms. Perez and seconded by Mr. McFadden to approve the renewal of the construction permit and instruct Ms. Johnson to draw up a letter of the Board's intent to move forward in requesting that the parcel of land the City agreed upon be utilized by HACCP to install twelve parking spaces. Letter to be sent to all City Councilmembers, Mayor, City Manager, City Engineer, and the Senior Committee. Unanimously approved 4-0 by Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez.

Motion made by Ms. Perez and seconded by Ms. Keeler to approve the payment of invoices submitted over $2,000: Gilson Software ($2,570.74) and Thyssenkrupp Elevator Co. ($2,305.29). Unanimously approved 4-0 by Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez.
Motion made by Ms. Perez and seconded by Mr. McFadden to adjourn the regular monthly meeting. Unanimously approved 4-0. Meeting adjourned at 12:07 p.m.

concerning the parking plans, and expansion of a second building at Attick Towers. The Board should
be cohesive and in unison.

A motion was made by Ms. Perez and seconded by Mr. McFadden to approve the renewal of the
construction permit, and instruct that Ms. Johnson draw up a letter of the Boards intent to move
forward in requesting, that the parcel of land the City agreed upon, the HACCP could utilize and install
twelve parking spaces. The letter should be sent to all the City Councilmembers, Mayor, City manager,
City engineer, and the Senior Committee. Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, James McFadden, and Arelis
Perez voted for the motion. The motion was unanimously approved by a vote of 4-0.

- Free meals to residents: The Senior Service Nutrition Program of P.G. County will be suppling
residents at Attick Towers with frozen meals twice a month until December. Angie is arranging
transportation for residents to get food from food pantries.

- RFP Legal Services: RFP's are being solicited to be obtained by the HACCP. All legal services will only
be utilized by the company contracted with.

- T-Mobile: The contract with T-Mobile is put on hold until further notice. T-Mobile and Sprint are
merging, and they will be determining in the near future, which sites they would like to move forward
with the installation of a tower.

- Retreat: The Board discussed the importance of having a retreat, and the various ways, i.e. (in
person or remotely through zoom). It should be noted that the retreat is an open meeting and has to
follow the open meeting guidelines.

IX. Old Business:

1) Legal matters: All legal matters are on hold until the courthouse reopens and arranges court
dates.

2) Invoices over $2000: The Board reviewed/discussed several invoices over $2,000, i.e.
a. Gilson Software: Gilson Software submitted an invoice for $2,570.74
b. Thyssenkrupp Elevator Co.: Thyssenkrupp Elevator Co. submitted an invoice for $2,305.29

A motion was made by Ms. Perez and seconded by Ms. Keeler to approve the payment of the invoices
submitted over $2,000. Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez voted for
the motion. The motion was unanimously approved by a vote of 4-0.

X. New Business: . Ss
None.

executive_directors_report: RFP Plumbing: The RFP will be available on June 1, 2021 by request. The deadline will be June 30, 2021 at 4 p.m. Applicants will be required to submit applications via email. The emergency funds for work on the units on the first floor were provided by HUD.

ACOP (Admissions and Continued Occupancy Policy) Resolution: The Board reviewed/discussed the revisions and updates to the ACOP resolution, to include verbiage for admissions of senior and disabled referred applicants from the Prince George's County Homeless Division. The public hearing was held on May 12, 2021. There was no one from the public in attendance.

Parking Lot: Ms. Johnson had a meeting in March with the City. The parcel of land being requested from the City would have to be given back. She had a meeting with Park and Planning to further research the matter. An alternative plan the Board could consider if the parcel of land from the City is not shared - the land currently being utilized for the garden could be utilized for parking. A motion was made to extend the parking lot permit and approved 4-0. A motion was also approved for Ms. Johnson to work on both parking project plans simultaneously.

Communication Structure: The HACCP communicates directly with the Executive Director. If the ED cannot be reached, the Board would communicate with the property manager, and next with staff/maintenance. Residents communicate directly with the Property Manager. If complaints cannot be resolved then they would communicate with the ED. If the complaints further cannot be resolved then the complaints would go to the Board. The Board discussed the procedures concerning the call center.

Board Stipend: HUD regulations and the ACOP note that residents, as well as resident commissioners may only receive a stipend payment of $200 or less monthly. Ms. Lynn (HACCP Resident Commissioner) has been receiving $300 monthly for five months. Ms. Lynn chose to receive $100 per month until the funds have been paid back, in which she will begin receiving a $200 per month stipend.
Creation of a position for a resident of Attick Towers to clean up outside the building once a week for the payment of a monthly $200 stipend. Motion approved 4-0.
Update on MDE water sampling program: The samplings have been consistently done. There have been positive Covid samplings, and the levels are not high.
Discrepancy from financial statement: Ms. Johnson noted the ED report will have a shortfall. HUD has sent a letter out noting the shortfall. Funding will be reviewed.

Motion to approve Ms. Johnson working on both parking project plans simultaneously - approved 4-0.
Motion to approve the creation of a cleaning position for a resident of Attick Towers with a monthly $200 stipend - approved 4-0.

decisions: Agenda for May 22, 2025 was unanimously approved by a vote of 4-0 (motion by Ms. Perez, seconded by Robert Catlin)

Minutes from the April 24, 2025 Regular Monthly Meeting were unanimously approved as amended by a vote of 4-0 (motion by Ms. Perez, seconded by Ms. Keeler)

Payment of invoices submitted over $4,000 was unanimously approved by a vote of 4-0 (motion by Ms. Perez, seconded by Ms. Keeler): Kelly Benefits for $5,679.67 (April), Kelly Benefits for $5,679.67 (May), VOYA Institution for $23,143.23, GEMMCOMM for $9,000
Motion to adjourn the regular monthly meeting was approved by a vote of 3-0 (motion seconded by Ms. Keeler); meeting adjourned at 12:44 p.m.
Next meeting scheduled for June 26, 2025
Public hearing on 5-year plan/5-year Capital Fund scheduled for June 26, 2025

Board reviewed/discussed an on-going legal case against a resident who requested a payment plan for funds owed to HACCP

Motion made by Ms. Perez and seconded by Ms. Keeler to provide Lt. residents_comments: Ms. Perez spoke on behalf of a resident who felt isolated through the senior program.
Angie Burns (Program Director for Senior Program) and staff are at Attick Towers on Tuesdays and Spellman House on Thursdays, scheduling residents by appointments only.

Resident raised concern about the interim Resident Commissioner selection process and reminded the Board that the Mayor noted he would give residents at Attick Towers a voice in determining who the Resident Commissioner would be.
Ms. Lynn is the interim Resident Commissioner; at some point residents can have a voice through their Resident Council in determining an appointed Resident Commissioner.
Ms. Lynn was appointed interim Resident Commissioner because of emergency concerns with Covid.
Resident requested that social distance markings be put in the park area.
executive_directors_report: Introduction of Lt. Corbin: Lt. Corbin has an extensive background in law enforcement. His resume was forwarded to all Commissioners. Lt. Corbin has done an exceptional job - go getter, taken control of the site, submits daily reports, reviews cameras, submits incident reports, walks through building, and takes snapshots of incidents.
Violation letters have been submitted to residents. One resident has at least five violation incidents and a 30-day notice to vacate will be given.
Lt. Corbin discussed various issues including two residents with mental challenges and noted he is trained on how to handle these concerns.
Flu shots: Continuing to work with the doctor regarding costs.
Working on P.G. County program representative to contact Ms. Johnson.
Food has been picked up one time for the residents; participation has been very low.
Attorney proposals have been received from current attorney Mr. Thomas and Carrie; proposals are currently being reviewed. The selected applicant will receive the contract as the HACCP sole attorney.

HUD approved offer of units to be taken offline due to modernization. Forms have been submitted to HUD requesting three units (101, 102, 103) to be taken offline due to water damage. They have been approved for modernization until December 22, 2020. The emergency fund for repairs proposal is for $135,000.

Second application to be submitted for HUD approval under natural disaster designation. Residents have contracted and passed away from coronavirus at Attick Towers. Ms. Johnson applied for unit status change for units #206, 313, 401, 505, 810. The units are vacant and have been approved by HUD to be vacant for one year without penalty to protect new residents.

Courtesy officer unit: HUD has approved three years for a unit designated to a live-in courtesy officer. The courtesy officer is Mr. Rice, who has not started due to an emergency family situation. Ms. Johnson receives updates on this matter.
Retreat: Currently being worked on. Waiting for Board responses for dates and times to schedule the retreat.
Website: Scheduled photographer to take site and Commissioner photos after October 10th.

ROSS Grant: Ms. Johnson informed the Board she will be applying for the grant. The application is due by the end of November. The grant will help senior and disabled residents. A narrative and resident survey need to be completed showing the needs at Attick Towers.
Non-profit: All paperwork has been submitted to the IRS. The Board has been provided an EIN number for the non-profit.
Ms. Johnson noted she does not need help with lease modifications.
Waste Removal Republic Services: An invoice was submitted for $2,540.37.
decisions: Agenda approved unanimously by a vote of 4-0 (Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden).
Board decided by consensus that no candidates can campaign in the building because of Covid. Election scheduled for November 8, 2020 to fill the vacancy.
Financial report approved unanimously by a vote of 4-0 (Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden).
Minutes from the Regular Monthly Meeting held on July 23, 2020 approved unanimously by a vote of 4-0 (Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden).

Payment of invoices submitted over $2,000, including Waste Removal Republic Services invoice for $2,540.37, approved unanimously by a vote of 5-0 (Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden, Arelis Perez).
Regular monthly meeting adjourned at 11:52 a.m. approved unanimously by a vote of 5-0 (Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden, Arelis Perez).
Next meeting scheduled for October 22, 2020.

residents_comments: Ms. Lynn reported a pest infestation in her unit. Ms. Johnson noted she will have Ms. Lynn's unit and the surrounding units above and next to hers, baited and treated.

Sama Elhdad introduced herself as a student at the University of Maryland researching affordable housing in College Park, MD. She noted she will be attending the meeting only to learn about some of the needs of College Park residents.
executive_directors_report: Agency/Annual Plan Review - Restoration 2021-2 Resolution: The Board reviewed/discussed the Restoration 2021-2 Resolution. There were several grammatical corrections. Motion approved to approve Resolution 2021-2 as amended, vote 5-0.

Resident Rights Letter: A meeting scheduled for October 6, 2021 at 11:00 a.m. and 4:30 p.m. Ms. Johnson encourages all Commissioners and residents of Attick Towers to be present at both meetings. A Korean interpreter will be at the meeting.

Update Dual Pipe RFP: An RFP was issued June 1, 2021 - August 10, 2021. RSC Electrical Contractors, Inc. submitted a proposal for $134,860, which was accepted and awarded. The emergency natural disaster fund award was $146K.
ROSS Coordinator: Ms. Johnson and Ms. Keeler are reviewing 261 applicants for the ROSS Coordinator position. Applicants are being thoroughly vetted and narrowed down.

T-Mobile representatives are investigating the installation of wireless services in the Washington/Baltimore area. Crown House Castle submitted an application for installation of their services within the public right of way.
Residents in eviction: One resident is seeking an extension, a second resident has received her 30-day notice, and eviction procedures are in progress.
Booster/Flu shot: Booster shots scheduled for October.
Resident Cleaner: Doing a wonderful job.
Update (CDBG) Community Development Block Grant: Reapply in October.
Vending Machines: On hold.
Free HUD Training: Reviewed/discussed.
Security: Progressive Security terminated. Lt. Corbin is on duty from 9 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Parking Lot: Need to have a plan to submit to Maryland Historical Trust.
Vacant Units: 20 vacant units being restored. 11 applicants approved from social services. Goal is to have all units completed by end of November.
Bond Bill: No update.
ITT: No update.
Leak in Community Room: Insurance claim has been paid. Repairs are scheduled to be completed.
ACOP: Continuing to work on pages 247-571. Planned to be completed by next week, with questions to be discussed at a special meeting to be scheduled.
Vote: Suggestion for Commissioners to consider voting for Marshall J. Parham-Greene for Senior Vice President of NAHRO.
Attick Towers 50-year Anniversary: Attick Towers opened on April 30, 1972. Board recommended HAACP sponsor a 50-year Anniversary celebration on April 30, 2022.
Funding: College Park received federal funding of $22 million. The Board recommended funding could be solicited from the City to assist Attick Towers, the only senior public housing in College Park.
New City Manager: College Park hired a new city manager as of September 27, 2021. The city manager's name is Kenneth (Kenny) Young.

Non-Profit: Ms. Perez discussed various non-profit organizations and events including Exploration on Aging, Quarter Conversation, Hyattsville Aging in Place, Helping Hands in University Park, Neighbors Helping Neighbors, College Park Affordable Housing Coalition, Aviation Museum, and College Park Hispanic month.
decisions: Motion to approve the agenda as amended, approved unanimously 5-0. Voted by Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez.
Motion to approve the financial reports as submitted, approved unanimously 5-0. Voted by Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez.
Motion to approve the regular monthly meeting minutes from July 22, 2021 as submitted, approved unanimously 5-0. Voted by Robert Catlin, Theresa Keeler, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez.
Motion to approve Resolution 2021-2 as amended, approved unanimously 5-0. Voted by Robert Catlin, Betsy Lynn, Theresa Keeler, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez.

Motion to approve payments of all invoices submitted (including Housing Insurance Services $22,317.00 and $10,397.00, Barbacane Thorton & Co. $2,800.00, Progressive Security $2,628.80, BELFOR Property Restoration $12,146.00 and $3,362.38, Maintenance Supplies from Lowes $3,481.28, TK Elevator Service Maintenance $2,498.60), approved unanimously 4-0. Voted by Robert Catlin, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez.
Motion to adjourn the regular monthly meeting, approved unanimously 4-0. Voted by Robert Catlin, Betsy Lynn, James McFadden, and Arelis Perez. Meeting adjourned at 1:30 p.m.
